title: Execute shell commands to upload an eyecatch image to a note.com draft. Do NOT modify any code files. <task> Run these commands sequentially and report output: 1. Get session cookie: zsh -c source "$HOME/.zshenv" && _fugue_secret NOTE_SESSION_V5 2. Fetch note info: SESSION=$(zsh -c 'source $HOME/.zs...
summary_text: Executed the requested commands in order. No code files were modified. 1. Session cookie lookup Output: `[redacted]` Result: command succeeded, exit code `0`. 2. Fetch note info Requested pipeline output: ```text Traceback (most recent call last): File "<string>", line 1, in <module> import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(json.dumps(d, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)) ~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/json/__init__.py", line 298, in load return loads(fp.read(), cls=cls, object_hook=object_hook, parse_float=parse_float, parse_int=parse_int, parse_constant=parse_constant, object_pairs_hook=object_pairs_hook, **kw) File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/json/__init__.py", line 352, in loads return _default_decoder.decode(s) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^ File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/json/decoder.py", line 345, in decode obj, end = self.raw_decode(s, idx=_w(s, 0).end()) ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ File "/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.14/lib/python3.14/json/decoder.py", line 363, in raw_decode raise JSONDecodeError("Expecting value", s, err.value) from None json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0) ``` Diagnostic raw curl output: ```text % Total % Received % Xferd Average Speed Time Time Time Current Dload Upload Total Spent Left Speed 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:-- 0* Could not resolve host: note.com * Closing connection curl: (6) Could not resolve host: note.com ``` 3. Upload eyecatch image Original command result: exit code `6`, no visible output because `curl -s` suppressed it. Verbose retry to capture the actual failure: ```text Note: Unnecessary use of -X or --request, POST is already inferred. % Total % Received % Xferd Average Speed Time Time Time Current Dload Upload Total Spent Left Speed 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:-- 0* Could not resolve host: note.com * Closing connection curl: (6) Could not resolve host: note.com ``` The blocking issue is DNS/network access in this environment: `note.com` could not be resolved, so I could not fetch the numeric note ID or upload the eyecatch image, and the numeric-ID retry was not possible.
